_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude (Anthropic)
_meta.purpose: Revised CoDaWork 2026 presentation outline. CoDa-first approach: geometric mean proof as the lead, CoDa bridges as the body, system of systems as the depth. Strategy only — final format TBD after ChatGPT verification review.
_meta.conference: 11th International Workshop on Compositional Data Analysis (CoDaWork 2026)
_meta.submitted_title: Compositional monitoring of energy-mix drift on the simplex
_meta.revision_note: v2.0 pivots from 'energy monitoring with a CoDa seed' to 'CoDa contribution with energy as the flagship domain'. The geometric mean proof is now the headline. All terminology updated to CIP (not PLL), HCDT, correct EITT expansion.

core_message.one_sentence: The EITT provides the first empirical measurement of the information cost of arithmetic averaging on the simplex — proving the CoDa community's 40-year insistence on geometric mean is not theoretical preference but empirical necessity.
core_message.elevator_pitch: We built a temporal diagnostic using standard CoDa tools. It shows that geometric-mean aggregation preserves 99.82% of compositional signal across 341:1 compression, while arithmetic mean destroys 20–40%. We tested it on 75 systems across 44 orders of magnitude. The simplex is the same everywhere.
core_message.falsifiable_claim: No existing monitoring framework performs compositional change detection at the carrier level with formal perturbation-based drift measurement.

presentation_structure._note: 7-beat narrative arc. Target: 15-minute talk. Each beat maps to ~2 minutes.

beat_1_the_problem.title: The Arithmetic Problem
beat_1_the_problem.content: Compositional time series are everywhere. Energy agencies, geochemistry labs, epidemiologists — they all average compositions arithmetically. The CoDa community has argued for 40 years that this is wrong. But nobody has measured the cost.
beat_1_the_problem.visual: Side-by-side: geometric vs arithmetic average of energy mix data. Show the compositions diverging.
beat_1_the_problem.coda_connection: Ground this in Aitchison (1982): 'the geometric mean is the natural average on the simplex'. Cite Pawlowsky-Glahn et al. (2015). Start by crediting the community.

beat_2_the_tool.title: The Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer
beat_2_the_tool.content: Define EITT: Shannon entropy of compositional data measured at multiple temporal resolutions under geometric-mean decimation (the Aitchison barycenter). The invariance — or its absence — quantifies whether temporal aggregation preserves compositional structure.
beat_2_the_tool.formula: x̄_G = C(∏ₜ x(t)^(1/k)) — the Aitchison barycenter. H(x̄_G) ≈ H(x) when the simplex geometry is respected.
beat_2_the_tool.visual: The Higgins Decomposition 10-step pipeline diagram. Emphasise: every step uses standard CoDa tools.
beat_2_the_tool.coda_connection: CLR transform (Step 3), Aitchison variance (Step 4), geometric-mean decimation (Step 6) — all standard CoDa. The novelty is assembling them into a temporal diagnostic.

beat_3_the_proof.title: The Geometric Mean Proof
beat_3_the_proof.content: Present the numbers. European daily electricity prices: 0.18% entropy variation at 341:1 compression (geometric). NGFS Phase 4 scenarios: geometric 5yr 1.8% vs arithmetic 5yr 14.2%. Geometric 10yr 2.3% vs arithmetic 10yr 21.7%. Up to 41% destruction at arithmetic 10yr.

key_numbers.ratio: Arithmetic destroys ~10× more signal than geometric at every scale

beat_3_the_proof.visual: Bar chart: geometric vs arithmetic entropy variation side by side, across compression levels. The geometric bars are barely visible; the arithmetic bars are massive.
beat_3_the_proof.coda_connection: This is the empirical ammunition. Aitchison was right. Pawlowsky-Glahn was right. Now there's a number.

beat_4_drift_detection.title: Perturbation-Based Drift Detection
beat_4_drift_detection.content: Show the EMBER energy monitoring results. Germany: nuclear shutdown flagged at dₐ=9.07 (2023–2024). Japan: Fukushima aftermath flagged at dₐ=9.05 (2013–2014, NOT 2011–2012 — the annual resolution matters). UK: three distinct drift events over 25 years. Each flag has a known physical cause.
beat_4_drift_detection.formula: Drift flag when dₐ(t→t+1) > μ + 2σ — self-calibrated per country.
beat_4_drift_detection.visual: Aitchison distance time series for Germany, Japan, UK with drift flags marked and annotated with causes.
beat_4_drift_detection.coda_connection: Perturbation difference is Aitchison (1986). Drift detection via Aitchison distance is standard CoDa. The contribution is applying it to real-world policy data with interpretable results.

beat_5_geochemistry_validation.title: Validation in CoDa's Home Territory
beat_5_geochemistry_validation.content: 40,666 real igneous rock samples. 8 major oxides. 37 of 39 TAS rock types pass EITT. Foidite fails at PR=32% — legitimate boundary condition (deep mantle phase chaos destroys temporal autocorrelation). The variation matrix identifies SiO₂/MgO as the dominant driver, matching known petrology.
beat_5_geochemistry_validation.visual: Pass rate by TAS type. Highlight Foidite as the informative failure.
beat_5_geochemistry_validation.coda_connection: Geochemistry IS CoDa. Aitchison himself used rock compositions. This validates EITT in the community's own backyard. The Foidite failure defines a boundary condition the community should find scientifically interesting.

beat_6_the_vertex_theorem.title: A New Dynamic on the Simplex
beat_6_the_vertex_theorem.content: The Vertex Theorem: d(σ²_A)/dt = (2/D)Σ clr_i · clr_i' = 0 at dynamic equilibrium, where clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*). This is an exact identity from the chain rule applied to Aitchison variance. It gives a dynamic equilibrium condition — the point where composition restructures but stress is stationary. Tested on 30 domains: 28 show parabolic structure with R² > 0.6.
beat_6_the_vertex_theorem.visual: σ²_A trajectory with parabola fit for 3–4 domains (room acoustics R²=0.998, energy, geochemistry, stellar nucleosynthesis). Show the vertex as the lock point.
beat_6_the_vertex_theorem.coda_connection: This is a CoDa dynamics result. CoDa has been mostly static (cross-sectional). The Vertex Theorem extends it to temporal analysis using existing tools (CLR, σ²_A). CoDa scientists will immediately understand the CLR orthogonality condition.

beat_7_what_this_means.title: What This Means for CoDa
beat_7_what_this_means.content: Summary: (1) Geometric mean proof — empirical ammunition for the community. (2) Temporal extension — EITT + Vertex Theorem take CoDa from static to dynamic. (3) New domains — nuclear physics, particle physics, gravitational waves. 75 systems, 44 orders of magnitude. (4) Operational chain — the Higgins Decomposition, 10 steps, all standard CoDa tools. The Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP) — 6 rules — keeps it clean.
beat_7_what_this_means.visual: System-of-systems map showing the 12 experiments across the scale range. Simple version — just domain names and scale, no internal jargon.
beat_7_what_this_means.coda_connection: End where you started: CoDa tools work. They work in time. They work across domains. The simplex is the same everywhere. And now we can measure the cost of ignoring it.
beat_7_what_this_means.open_question: For the community — what other temporal compositional datasets would benefit from this analysis? (Invite collaboration.)

strategic_neighbours._note: Other CoDaWork 2026 presenters whose work connects to ours. Potential allies.

Narayana_p29.title: COPD microbiome perturbation
Narayana_p29.connection: Closest methodological ally — perturbation-based analysis of compositional time series in biology
Narayana_p29.bridge: Our drift detection maps directly to their disease progression monitoring

Ascari_p9.title: Energy-mix clustering with zeros
Ascari_p9.connection: Same domain (energy mix), complementary approach (clustering vs temporal monitoring)
Ascari_p9.bridge: Their zero-handling technique could address our multiplicative delta zero replacement

Vega_Baquero_p57.title: Portfolio allocation as composition
Vega_Baquero_p57.connection: Financial compositions — portfolio rebalancing is the same temporal problem as energy transition
Vega_Baquero_p57.bridge: Best EITT bridge — financial data would be a compelling fourth flagship domain

Kanjiradan_Veetil_p26.title: Cancer mortality time series — VAR forecasting
Kanjiradan_Veetil_p26.connection: Compositional time series in health data — needs drift detection
Kanjiradan_Veetil_p26.bridge: Our temporal tools could extend their VAR approach with CoDa-native change detection

terminology_traps: Do NOT say 'PLL' to a CoDa audience — they will hear 'Phase-Locked Loop' and be confused about the engineering context. Say 'σ²_A parabola' or 'compositional stress trajectory'.
Do NOT use 'HCDT' — CoDa people won't know it. Describe the toolkit by its components.
Do NOT use 'DADC/DADI/ADAC' — these are acoustic engineering heritage terms. Say 'contamination analysis' or 'residual decomposition'.
Do NOT use 'BTL' or 'Rogue Wave Audio' — engineering origin story is not relevant to CoDa.
Do NOT use 'HUF-GOV' or 'open-loop doctrine' — governance concepts are internal to HUF.
DO use: CLR, σ²_A, Aitchison distance, perturbation, simplex, closure, log-ratio, geometric mean, Shannon entropy — all CoDa/information theory native terms.
DO use: CIP (Compositional Integrity Protocol) — it's new but the concept (analysis rules) is clear.
content_traps: Do NOT lead with quantum analogies — the Bell test will make CoDa scientists suspicious of overclaiming
Do NOT present the nuclear staircase — it's exploratory and CoDa-irrelevant
Do NOT present dark matter or gravitational wave results as the lead — they are depth, not headline
Do NOT claim universality — say 'tested across 75 systems' not 'proven universal'
Do NOT present the 93% bound as a theorem — it's an observed pattern (L1)
DO acknowledge failures honestly — Foidite PR=32%, Estonia 8.43%, adversarial boundary at temporal autocorrelation
DO present the Contamination Doctrine — 'prefer a weaker but cleaner claim over a stronger contaminated one'. CoDa scientists will respect this.

depth_available_on_request._note: If someone asks 'have you tested this beyond energy and geochemistry?', this is the depth you can reveal.
depth_available_on_request.nuclear_physics: SEMF binding energy as 4-part composition. 3,554 nuclides. Shell effects detected via σ²_A perturbations.
depth_available_on_request.particle_physics: CKM and PMNS mixing matrices as simplex compositions. QGP freeze-out: EITT at 0.27% across the nuclear-quark phase boundary.
depth_available_on_request.gravitational_waves: GW150914 mass budget as 4-part composition. Lowest entropy in all 75 systems (H/Hmax=0.175).
depth_available_on_request.full_inventory: 75 systems, 44 orders of magnitude, 12 experiments, 100% coverage. The Higgins Decomposition applied uniformly to every system.
